get.description: Get values for asset data model parameters given in query parameter.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: Asset identity

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description:
List of data model parameters to retrieve.
If parameters list is empty then complete data model is returned.
Data model path parts should be dot-separated.
Parameter name should not contain the root object, for example use *AssetSummary* instead *InternetGatewayAsset.AssetSummary*.
Query for all sub-parameters of given object should end with dot ".", for example: parameters=Location.

parameters.name: exactId
parameters.description: Parameter indicating whether provided id is used as an exact asset identifier even when there are assets with the same serial number in the system.

parameters.name: deduplicationQuery
parameters.description:
Query used only when there is more than one asset with the same serial number in the system. The query is used to select one asset from a pool of duplicates.


 Comma separated list of conditions. Conditions are concatenated using *AND* logical operator.
 Condition has form: *fieldName operator value*
 ## Available fields and operators:
  - **id** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **restricted** (boolean): eq
 - **onboardingActive** (boolean): eq
 - **lastLinkTime** (string($date-time)): in, gte, notexists, exists, lte, gt, eq, ne, nin, lt
 - **lastProvisionTime** (string($date-time)): in, gte, notexists, exists, lte, gt, eq, ne, nin, lt
 - **lastRebootTime** (string($date-time)): in, gte, notexists, exists, lte, gt, eq, ne, nin, lt
 - **creationTime** (string($date-time)): in, gte, notexists, exists, lte, gt, eq, ne, nin, lt
 - **networkAddress** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **registrationCode** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **vendorPrefix** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **variant** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **firmwareRevision** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **appRevision** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **category** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **vendor** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **description** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **directClusters** ([string]): contains, notcontains, containsany
 - **clusters** ([string]): contains, notcontains, containsany
 - **properties.*** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt

 ## Value types:
 - *string* - surround with single quotes: '. Escape single quotes in queries with another single quote: ''. E.g. 'Cite: ''cited text''.'.
 - *boolean* - true or false
 - *string($date-time)* - string in the ISO8601 format
 - *string($byte)* - base64 encoded string
 - *numeric* - for example: 0.123, 123, 12.123
 - *[Value type]* - array of value types. Array has the form of: [item1, item2, ...]
 - *object* - JSON object wrapped in backtick: \`. Escape backtick inside JSON string with another backtick: \`\`. E.g. \`{"backtick": "\`\`"}\`

 ## Operators:
  - **eq** - Field value is equal to condition value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **ne** - Field value is different than condition value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **in** - Field value is equal to one of the given values. Condition Value Type = [Field Type]
 - **nin** - Field value is different than all given values. Condition Value Type = [Field Type]
 - **exists** - Field does exists. Condition does not have value and has form *fieldName operator*
 - **notexists** - Field does not exists. Condition does not have value and has form *fieldName operator*
 - **gt** - Field value is greater than given value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **gte** - Field value is greater or equal to given value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **lt** - Field value is lower than given value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **lte** - Field value is lower or equal to given value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **contains** - Field value contains the given value. Applicable only to collection fields. Condition Value Type = Field Item Type
 - **notcontains** - Field value not contains the given value. Applicable only to collection fields. Condition Value Type = Field Item Type
 - **containsany** - Field value contains any of the given values. Applicable only to collection fields. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **strcontains** - Field value contains given substring. Applicable only to string fields. Condition Value Type = string
 - **matches** - Field value matches given regular expression. Applicable only to string fields. Condition Value Type = string
 - **startswith** - Field value starts with given string. Applicable only to string fields. Condition Value Type = string
 - **endswith** - Field value ends with given string. Applicable only to string fields. Condition Value Type = string

 ## Examples:
 - *someStringField eq 'string'*
 - *someDateCollection containsany ['2012-09-01T08:51:23.180Z', '2012-09-02T08:51:23.180Z']*
 - *someMapField.keyName eq 'value'*
 - *someBooleanField eq true*

post.description:
Update IP address and joinRequestUrl attribute. Asset with given MAC is updated.
No specific format of input MAC address required, i.e. 01:23:45:67:89:AB or 0123.4567.89ab will both be matched to 0123456789AB MAC.
It's important to ensure that MAC addresses used in Platform are in uppercase format.

post.description:
Create or update existing workspace entity.

Workspace identity consist of the root workspace name `/` followed by any number of sub-workspace names.
A sub-workspace name consists of any number of alphanumeric or '_' characters followed by '/'.

For example workspace identity: `/workspace/sub_workspace/` consists of 3 workspace names:
 `/` which is parent of `workspace/` which is parent of `sub_workspace/`.

If posted workspace identity contains nonexistent ancestor then ancestor hierarchy will be created automatically.
Whenever a new workspace is created, a cluster will be automatically created and linked to it.

get.description: Get online assets in workspace count.
Get workspace statistics by identity.
Get workspace by identity.

delete.description:
Delete workspace entity.
This operation will fail if the workspace has sub-workspaces unless recursively is set to true.
In that case the workspace will be deleted together with all its sub-workspaces.
Clusters and dependencies should be deleted first before deleting workspaces.

Cluster identity consists of dot-separated all parent clusters names and the cluster name, staring form root cluster.
Workspace name consists of slash-separated all parent workspace names and the workspace name, starting from / workspace
Workspace field is optional, and if not given, account's workspace is used
Example request body:
{
  "id": "root.parent1.parent2.clusterName",
  "workspace": "/parentWorkspace/yourWorkspace/",
  "description": "cluster description"
}
When cluster id with not existing parent cluster names is given then parent clusters are created automatically.

 - **active** (boolean): eq
 - **description** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **condition** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt

 ## Value types:
 - *string* - surround with single quotes: '. Escape single quotes in queries with another single quote: ''. E.g. 'Cite: ''cited text''.'.

 - *string($byte)* - base64 encoded string

 - *[Value type]* - array of value types. Array has the form of: [item1, item2, ...]

  - **eq** - Field value is equal to condition value. Condition Value Type = Field Type

 - **nin** - Field value is different than all given values. Condition Value Type = [Field Type]

 - **notexists** - Field does not exists. Condition does not have value and has form *fieldName operator*

 - **gte** - Field value is greater or equal to given value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **lt** - Field value is lower than given value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **lte** - Field value is lower or equal to given value. Condition Value Type = Field Type

 - **notcontains** - Field value not contains the given value. Applicable only to collection fields. Condition Value Type = Field Item Type
 - **containsany** - Field value contains any of the given values. Applicable only to collection fields. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **strcontains** - Field value contains given substring. Applicable only to string fields. Condition Value Type = string
 - **matches** - Field value matches given regular expression. Applicable only to string fields. Condition Value Type = string
 - **startswith** - Field value starts with given string. Applicable only to string fields. Condition Value Type = string
 - **endswith** - Field value ends with given string. Applicable only to string fields. Condition Value Type = string

 ## Examples:
 - *someStringField eq 'string'*
 - *someDateCollection containsany ['2012-09-01T08:51:23.180Z', '2012-09-02T08:51:23.180Z']*

get.description: Get tracking configuration.

Get summaryd numerical artifact data.
The time span of single returned summary depends on requested time range (fromDate, toDate):
less than one day - minute summarys, less than one month - hour summarys, otherwise - day summarys.
Default fromDate value is one day before toDate.
Default toDate value is current time.

Get summaryd textual artifact data.
The time span of single returned summary depends on requested time range (fromDate, toDate):
less than one day - minute summarys, less than one month - hour summarys, otherwise - day summarys.
Default fromDate value is one day before toDate.
Default toDate value is current time.
Get next metric batch for cursor value obtained from GET on /metrics or previous GET on /moreMetrics

Get cursor for all metrics for given tracking within given time period.
Default fromDate value is one day before toDate.
Default toDate value is current time.

parameters.name: returnMatchedAsset
parameters.description: Parameter indicating whether the endpoint should find and return a matched asset. Disclaimer: if it is set to true, a query to a asset collection will be made to the properties.key field, which may cause performance issues. Also, if it is frequently used, consider contacting AVSystem to ensure that an additional index is added to optimize this query.

put.description:
Create or Edit AttributeService. 
If a AttributeService already exists, any matching properties will be updated with the provided values.
Any properties that exist in service, but are not included in the request will be set to null. 
If a AttributeService does not exist, a new one will be created. 
Include the workspace name within slashes (example: /workspace/) when making the request.

parameters.description:
Query used only when there is more than one asset with the same serial number in the system. The query is used to select one asset from a pool of duplicates.

 - **registrationCode** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **vendorPrefix** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **variant** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **firmwareRevision** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **appRevision** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **category** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **vendor** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **description** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt
 - **directClusters** ([string]): contains, notcontains, containsany
 - **clusters** ([string]): contains, notcontains, containsany
 - **properties.*** (string): in, gte, startswith, endswith, exists, lte, matches, gt, eq, notexists, strcontains, ne, nin, lt

 ## Value types:
 - *string* - surround with single quotes: '. Escape single quotes in queries with another single quote: ''. E.g. 'Cite: ''cited text''.'.

 - *string($date-time)* - string in the ISO8601 format
 - *string($byte)* - base64 encoded string
 - *numeric* - for example: 0.123, 123, 12.123
 - *[Value type]* - array of value types. Array has the form of: [item1, item2, ...]
 - *object* - JSON object wrapped in backtick: \`. Escape backtick inside JSON string with another backtick: \`\`. E.g. \`{"backtick": "\`\`"}\`

 ## Operators:
  - **eq** - Field value is equal to condition value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **ne** - Field value is different than condition value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **in** - Field value is equal to one of the given values. Condition Value Type = [Field Type]
 - **nin** - Field value is different than all given values. Condition Value Type = [Field Type]
 - **exists** - Field does exists. Condition does not have value and has form *fieldName operator*
 - **notexists** - Field does not exists. Condition does not have value and has form *fieldName operator*
 - **gt** - Field value is greater than given value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **gte** - Field value is greater or equal to given value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **lt** - Field value is lower than given value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **lte** - Field value is lower or equal to given value. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **contains** - Field value contains the given value. Applicable only to collection fields. Condition Value Type = Field Item Type
 - **notcontains** - Field value not contains the given value. Applicable only to collection fields. Condition Value Type = Field Item Type
 - **containsany** - Field value contains any of the given values. Applicable only to collection fields. Condition Value Type = Field Type
 - **strcontains** - Field value contains given substring. Applicable only to string fields. Condition Value Type = string
 - **matches** - Field value matches given regular expression. Applicable only to string fields. Condition Value Type = string
 - **startswith** - Field value starts with given string. Applicable only to string fields. Condition Value Type = string
 - **endswith** - Field value ends with given string. Applicable only to string fields. Condition Value Type = string

 ## Examples:
 - *someStringField eq 'string'*
 - *someDateCollection containsany ['2012-09-01T08:51:23.180Z', '2012-09-02T08:51:23.180Z']*
 - *someMapField.keyName eq 'value'*
 - *someBooleanField eq true*

post.description:
Create job that will be executed on asset with given identity.
Task definition will be copied from job blueprint with given name.
Some values from blueprint fields can be overwritten in given TaskConfig.
If webhook is registered, the TaskOutcomeDTO will be sent to it using HTTP POST each time job finishes execution.
The result of this method is created Job identity.

post.description:
Create job that will be executed on asset with given identity.
Link will be triggered to execute the job.
Task definition will be copied from job blueprint with given name.
Some values from blueprint fields can be overwritten in given TaskConfig.
This method will wait up to waitTimeSeconds for job execution and returns TaskOutcome.
If job will not execute in given time period the TaskOutcome with NotStarted status will be returned.
Optionally job can wait up to joinRequestWaitTimeSeconds for connection request to be executed.
If the connect request is not completed within this time, this method will exit immediately.
In case of connection request failed, or job time limit exceeded, the created job can be deleted when the parameter deleteTaskOnTimeout is true
If webhook is registered, the TaskOutcomeDTO will be sent to it using HTTP POST each time job finishes execution.

requestBody.description:
Pipeline request body can contain an arbitrary JSON object, i.e. 

{
  "stringKey": "stringValue",
  "booleanKey": true,
  "numberKey": 1,
  "arrayKey": [1, 2, 3],
  "objectKey": {
     "nestedStringKey": "nestedStringValue",
     "nestedNumber": 2
  }
}
The pipeline author decides on request body schema, required fields and their meaning.
